items.summary: ENGLAND supporters heading to the USA to support the Three Lions are set for a hefty bar bill. Many made the trip to Tampa's Raymond James stadium to watch Harry Kane and Co win a warm-up match against New Zealand on Saturday 1-0. items.summary: TOTTENHAM have had TWO bids rejected for £70million-rated Brighton defender Jan Paul van Hecke. Spurs chief Roberto De Zerbi is desperate to bolster his squad after only narrowly avoiding relegation from the Premier League. items.title: Iran says U.S. denied visas to key WC officials
items.summary: Iran's soccer federation has accused the United States of "vindictive behavior" after saying that visas were refused for "key managerial and administrative members" of its World Cup team.

items.title: Dembélé: France to end Deschamps' reign on top
items.summary: France winger Ousmane Dembele said the impending departure of manager Didier Deschamps is not a distraction for the squad heading into the FIFA World Cup, but is more of a motivator for the team to finish his reign on a high note.

items.title: Kane: Heat 'won't be a factor' at World Cup
items.summary: Harry Kane has said he thinks England's World Cup preparations means the much-publicised high temperatures at this summer's tournament "won't be a factor."

items.title: Wilson scores early but USWNT beaten in Brazil
items.summary: Sophia Wilson scored her first goal for the United States since 2024 but Brazil rallied to a 2-1 win in Saturday's international friendly in Sao Paulo.

items.title: Poch finally feels WC 'excitement' building in U.S...
items.summary: United States coach Mauricio Pochettino said he could finally feel the excitement he expected to see from the American fanbase during his team's 2-1 loss to Germany on Saturday at Soldier Field -- its final game before the start of the World Cup.

items.title: Messi left on bench as Argentina beat Honduras
items.summary: Argentina beat Honduras 2-0 on Saturday in their second-to-last friendly before the FIFA World Cup begins on June 11.
